acceptance_targets: [chapter_15_craft_001] In the revised aftermath spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:333 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:453, at least one character besides Della names a concrete harm still caused by her limit-setting, and neither that exchange nor the closing paragraph contains an explicit sentence declaring her choice right, necessary, or the only fit truth. A reader should leave the chapter with the cost still open, not morally settled.

[FBR-003] Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5293-5305 no longer claims Della brought or copied a fever-ground extract before the theft, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5499-5501 grounds the ruling only in evidence the manuscript has already established on-page. Fail if the hearing still depends on a copied fever-ground page or any other newly invented pre-theft document.

[FBR-004] Pass if the revised testimony at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5339-5347 matches the established south/below-cedar placement and no north-side grave coordinate remains in chapter_15. Fail if chapter_15 still asks the reader to reconcile incompatible grave locations between the hearing and the exhumation chapters.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised aftermath spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:333 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:453, at least one character besides Della names a concrete harm still caused by her limit-setting, and neither that exchange nor the closing paragraph contains an explicit sentence declaring her choice right, necessary, or the only fit truth. A reader should leave the chapter with the cost still open, not morally settled.
findings.evidence: The chapter repeatedly reassures the reader that Della's withholding was the right middle path instead of letting the damage stay jagged: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:341; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:359; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:377; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:441; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:453.
findings.problem: This lands as a moral safety valve, which the constitution treats as sanitization drift. After a chapter built on whether Della has the right to keep gatekeeping the valley's secrets, the hallway and truck aftermath repeatedly certify her choice as the necessary or nearest-just act. That reassurance blunts the ethical abrasion the chapter has earned and softens the continuing violence of her selective control over other people's histories.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the aftermath beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:333, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:359, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:377, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:441, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:453 so they do not function as explicit endorsement. Keep Noreen's and Rafe's concrete losses live on the page, and if Lucille speaks at all from those spans, make her stop at procedural fact or partial uncertainty rather than blessing Della's choice. Let the ending stay inside unresolved cost instead of thesis-level justification.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5293-5305 no longer claims Della brought or copied a fever-ground extract before the theft, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5499-5501 grounds the ruling only in evidence the manuscript has already established on-page. Fail if the hearing still depends on a copied fever-ground page or any other newly invented pre-theft document.
findings.problem: The hearing says Della brought three copied pages including "the fever ground" and then describes a copied page that already marks the row, cedar stump, collarbone, tooth, and burial night. Earlier chapters establish the opposite: at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3471-3473 Lucille states Della did not copy the fever-plot entry, and at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3505 the copied bundle is explicitly said to have a blank where Gabriel should have been. Chapter_15 therefore invents documentary support that the novel had already denied existed before the ledger theft.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the hearing setup at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5293-5305 and the ruling support at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5499-5501 so chapter_15 does not rely on a pre-theft copied fever-ground page. The scene can still carry the hearing on evidence the novel has already established exists: Della's sworn testimony, Silas's corroborating statement already filed, the exhumed collarbone and tooth, Gabriel's pocketwatch, and the parcel map. If you need paperwork in the room, make it the contest filing or exhumation notes rather than a copy chapter_09 says was never made.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised testimony at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5339-5347 matches the established south/below-cedar placement and no north-side grave coordinate remains in chapter_15. Fail if chapter_15 still asks the reader to reconcile incompatible grave locations between the hearing and the exhumation chapters.
findings.problem: Della's hearing testimony relocates Gabriel's grave to the "north side" of the lower fever ground and "six rows from the cedar stump," contradicting the placement already established in earlier chapters: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:717 places the grave below the lightning-scarred cedar and east of the child fever row, while snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4627-4631 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4713 place it on the south edge below the cedar root. Because grave placement is part of the identification logic, this contradiction undercuts the court scene's authority.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the testimony block at snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5339-5347 so Della uses the same burial geometry already established elsewhere in the novel. Keep one consistent coordinate system across the chapter: below the cedar/lightning-scarred cedar, east of the child row, south edge/below the cedar root. Do not leave "north side" in chapter_15 unless earlier chapters are also changed, which this workflow cannot do.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into morally compromised caretaking, the bureaucratic brutality of naming, and the physical labor of moving the dead. When an edge case appears, prefer the detail a witness would remember over the phrasing a polite narrator would use. Let tenderness exist inside harsh material without cleaning the harshness off.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If childbirth, exhumation, poverty, sexual history, bodily decay, or violence enters a scene, render it through specific sensation and consequence; summarizing away the hard fact counts as a failed pass, not tasteful restraint.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High overall; contractions are the norm across dialogue, with fuller forms reserved for class performance, legal formality, deliberate emphasis, or a character trying not to lose composure.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate public labor chapters with quieter domestic or inventory chapters, vary scene count and dialogue density, let some chapters turn on information or paperwork rather than confrontation, and ensure no two consecutive chapters share the same dominant mechanism, pace, or emotional temperature.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plainspoken historical Appalachian-Southern English without caricature; concrete, unsentimental, regionally grounded, and alert to how legal language clashes with lived speech.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third centered on Della Bledsoe, intimate enough to track bodily memory and evasive thought while never slipping into other characters' interior monologue as fact
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate muscular work-sentence compression with longer accumulating sentences that carry memory, atmosphere, and moral weight; let cadence tighten in hearings and exhumations, then widen in domestic or reflective passages.
